content: UX state best practices: use skeleton screens for content loads over ~500ms, spinners only for instant/short system actions; skeletons reduce perceived wait by showing structure first. Every click/submission needs immediate visual feedback. Empty states should never be literally empty: distinguish first-use (educate + primary CTA like 'Create your first X'), no-results (explain + clear filters), cleared, and error variants. Error states must say what went wrong in plain language, name the resolving action, and link to it. Hint/placeholder text must meet WCAG contrast; never rely on color alone.
